listen.description: To intentionally focus your attention on a sound or a speaker to understand what they are communicating

silent.description: A total absence of sound or a choice to refrain from speaking

enlist.description: To formally sign up or enroll, usually for military service, or to secure the help and support of someone for a cause

tinsel.description: A decorative material consisting of thin, shimmering strips of metal, plastic, or paper, traditionally draped over Christmas trees to create a glittering, icy effect

inlets.description: A geographical term for a narrow strip of water extending from a sea or lake into the land, or a passage between islands

alter.description: To change or modify something in some way, such as its size, shape, or character, without changing it into something entirely new

alert.description: A state of heightened watchfulness or a warning signal used to notify people of danger, an issue, or an important update

later.description: A time in the future or an event occurring after a present or previously mentioned time

ratel.description: Another name for the honey badger

taler.description: A historical silver coin widely used in German states between the 15th and 19th centuries
